Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The results show elevated HbA1c (6.3%) indicating prediabetes or early diabetes, with low fasting glucose possibly due to dietary factors or medication. LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated, increasing cardiovascular risk. Chloride is slightly high, but other electrolytes and renal function are normal. Hemoglobin is mildly elevated, which may warrant further evaluation. Platelet distribution width is high, but platelet count is normal. Overall, monitor glycemic control and lipid profile; consider further assessment for elevated hemoglobin.
Provider Organization: Facharzt für Allgemeinmedizin Johann Holbach
